segments.p1-7-g74: "Ведь" is a discourse particle meaning 'after all' that frames the speaker's appeal; "ваши выгоды" is a nominative plural noun phrase 'your benefits/advantages'; the dash plus "это" introduces a predicate nominative, common in Russian when equating a plural subject with an abstract singular predicate (here "благоденствие" = 'well-being').
segments.p1-7-g75: A continuation of the enumerative nominal list in the nominative: "богатство" stands as the next item ('wealth'), punctuated by a comma to separate list elements.
segments.p1-7-g76: Another nominative list item: "свобода" ('freedom')—simple noun in apposition to the initial predicate.
segments.p1-7-g77: Another list item in the same pattern: "покой" ('peace/quiet'), nominative singular completing the series of benefits.
segments.p1-7-g78: "Ну" is a pragmatic filler 'well'; "и так далее" is the set phrase 'and so on' ("и" linking, "так далее" = 'so further'), used here parenthetically to close the list.
segments.p1-7-g79: A repeated "и так далее" (again 'and so on') separated by a stronger punctuation mark; the repetition is rhetorical emphasis continuing the enumerative cadence.
segments.p1-7-g80: "Так что" is a result/consequence conjunction meaning 'so/therefore'; it introduces the consequence clause, and "человек" ('a man/person') is the subject of that following clause, set off by a comma before the relative clause.
segments.p1-7-g81: "Который" ('who/that') begins a relative clause modifying "человек"; the particle "бы" signals the conditional/subjunctive mood (often attached to the verb, but here placed after the pronoun), so the clause is hypothetical.
segments.p1-7-g82: "Например" is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'for example' that signals the following is an illustrative case.
segments.p1-7-g83: "Явно" ('openly/manifestly') and the coordinating "и" function as adverbial intensifiers; "зазнамо" is an archaic/colloquial adverb meaning 'arrogantly' or 'deliberately with a certain pride'. "Вошел" is the past tense masculine of войти ('to enter'), here used with "против" ('against') to mean 'to go/stand in opposition to'. "Всего этого реестра" is a genitive phrase 'of this whole register' ("всего" = 'whole/all of', "этого" = 'this', "реестра" = genitive of 'register').
segments.p1-7-g84: "Был бы" is the past tense verb "был" plus the particle "бы" forming the conditional ('would be'); here it serves as the main predicate of the hypothetical clause.
segments.p1-7-g85: "По-вашему" is a fixed prepositional expression meaning 'in your opinion' or 'according to you' ("по" with dative), marking that the judgment that follows is attributed to the addressees.
segments.p1-7-g86: "Ну да и" is a colloquial cluster of discourse particles: "ну" ('well'), "да" ('yes' or mild assent here), and "и" ('and')—together they hedge or soften the speaker's shift to his own view before the parenthetical remark.
segments.p1-7-g87: "Конечно" is a parenthetical adverb 'of course' reinforcing the following commitment; it marks strong agreement or inevitability.
segments.p1-7-g88: "По-моему" ('in my opinion') is another prepositional opinion phrase ("по" + dative) signalling the speaker's own stance immediately after reporting the others' view.
segments.p1-7-g89: Two nominal predicates linked by "или" ('or'): "обскурант" (a noun 'obscurantist' or reactionary anti-intellectual) and "совсем сумасшедший" ('completely/utterly mad'), where "совсем" is an adverb intensifier modifying the adjective "сумасшедший".
segments.p1-7-g90: "Так ли?" is a short tag/question: "так" = 'so' and the enclitic particle "ли" turns it into a confirming question ('is that so? / isn't it?'), inviting agreement or challenge.
segments.p1-7-g91: A cluster of small particles that sets up the following remark. Но introduces contrast ('but'); ведь adds an explanatory/emphatic 'after all'; вот draws the listener's attention ('here/now'); что begins the content clause ('what...'); удивительно is a predicative adjective meaning 'is surprising'. The colon signals that the speaker will state exactly what is surprising.
segments.p1-7-g92: Отчего opens an indirect question 'why/for what reason'; это is the anticipatory 'this/it' referring to the matter just announced; так modifies происходить to mean 'happen in that way'; происходит is 3rd-person singular present 'happens/is occurring.' Together: 'why is it that this happens.'
segments.p1-7-g93: Что here is the conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause (it links back to 'отчего... происходит, что ...'). Все эти is a demonstrative noun phrase 'all these' modifying статистики, the plural nominative 'statisticians' (actors of the verb that follows).
segments.p1-7-g94: A coordinated noun phrase in nominative listing other actors: мудрецы 'sages' and любители 'lovers/admiring advocates' (of) рода человеческого 'of the human kind/race' — рода is 'kind/type' in the genitive, человеческого qualifies it.
segments.p1-7-g95: При + noun in the locative (исчислении) marks the circumstance 'in/as regards the calculation of...'; исчислении is a noun meaning 'calculation/numbering'; человеческих выгод is the genitive 'of human advantages/benefits' that is the object of the process.
segments.p1-7-g96: Постоянно is an adverb 'constantly/always'; одну выгоду is the direct object in the accusative 'one (particular) advantage'; пропускают is 3rd-person plural present 'they omit/leave out.' The word order places 'one advantage' before the verb for emphasis, and the clause is a question by intonation.
segments.p1-7-g97: Даже и is an emphatic combination meaning 'not even' here; в расчёт не берут is an idiom 'do not take into account' (берут is present plural of брать, negated by не). Ее is the object pronoun 'it' (referring to the omitted advantage). В том виде is 'in the form/shape' (в + demonstrative том + виде).
segments.p1-7-g98: В каком begins a relative clause 'in which'; ее is again the object pronoun 'it'; следует is an impersonal/modal use meaning 'ought/should' here (the verb expresses necessity); брать is the infinitive 'to take' — together 'in which it ought to be taken.'
segments.p1-7-g99: А introduces a contrastive 'but/and'; от этого 'from this' (genitive) points to the consequence; и here adds emphasis ('and so'); весь расчёт 'the whole calculation' is the subject phrase; зависит is 3rd-person singular 'depends (on)'. The clause means 'and the whole calculation depends on that.'
segments.p1-7-g100: A short conditional clause: Беда 'trouble/misfortune' as subject; бы is the conditional particle 'would'; не велика is the short-form adjective 'not great.' Together they mean 'the trouble would not be great.'
segments.p1-7-g101: Взять бы — an infinitive plus the particle бы expressing a hypothetical or suggestion ('one might take/should take'); ее is the object pronoun 'it' (referring to the advantage). The phrase is an elliptical proposal: 'one could take it.'
segments.p1-7-g102: Эту выгоду is an explicit restatement in the accusative 'this advantage,' placed appositionally to clarify what 'it' refers to (reinforcing the object of 'взять').
segments.p1-7-g103: Да и is a colloquial connector meaning 'and besides/and also'; занести is an infinitive 'to enter/record' (here: 'to put on the list'); в список 'into the list' is the prepositional phrase marking the target. The clause completes the suggestion: 'and put it down on the list.'
segments.p1-7-g49: Что такое is a standard Russian formula for asking a definition — что (interrogative 'what') + такое (demonstrative/quality particle) — with the copula omitted. Выгода is nominative and the thing being defined; the literal structure is 'What such advantage?' → 'What is advantage?'.
segments.p1-7-g50: Да и is a colloquial connective meaning 'and/besides'; берете ли вы на себя is a present‑tense interrogative (берете ... ли) with на себя 'upon yourself' meaning 'undertake/take upon yourself'. Совершенно точно определить is an infinitive clause functioning as the object — two adverbs (совершенно, точно) modify the infinitive определить.
segments.p1-7-g51: В чем is the prepositional question 'in what'; именно gives emphasis 'exactly/precisely'; человеческая выгода is the noun phrase 'human advantage' (subject/theme); состоит is the present tense of состоять ('consist'), placed at the end as typical for Russian clauses with fronted question words.
segments.p1-7-g52: А introduces contrast; что если is the idiom 'what if' (introducing a hypothetical); так случится uses the perfective verb случиться in the future/hypothetical sense 'it happens/should happen that' (случится is 3rd person singular).
segments.p1-7-g53: Это subordinate clause begins with что ('that') followed by the noun phrase человеческая выгода ('human advantage') and the adverbial phrase иной раз ('at times/sometimes'); не только может sets up a 'not only may' construction whose continuation follows in the next segment (modal может = 'may/can').
segments.p1-7-g54: Но даже и is emphatic ('but even'); должна is the modal/predicative form of должный here meaning 'ought to' or 'must' (feminine singular to agree with выгода); именно в том состоять is an idiomatic infinitive phrase 'to consist precisely in that', where в том points back to the content of the subordinate clause.
segments.p1-7-g55: Чтоб introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that' / 'that'); в ином случае is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in another case' or 'otherwise'; себе is the dative reflexive 'to oneself'; худого пожелать uses the perfective infinitive пожелать ('to wish') with худого indicating the undesirable thing wished (here 'ill/bad fortune' — idiomatic), forming 'to wish oneself ill'.
segments.p1-7-g56: А не is the contrastive 'and not/rather than'; выгодного is the adjective in the same role as худого, 'advantageous/profitable', completing the contrast 'wish oneself ill rather than something advantageous'.
segments.p1-7-g57: А is a coordinating particle here ('and'/'but') used to introduce the conditional; если так literally means 'if so' or 'if that's the case' and sets up the hypothetical clause.
segments.p1-7-g58: This conditional expansion contains если только (literally 'if only' / 'if indeed'), followed by the impersonal modal phrase может быть ('may be' / 'might be'), with этот случай ('this case') as the potential instance; может here expresses possibility rather than ability, and быть is the infinitive 'to be' forming the modal construction.
segments.p1-7-g59: То is the result-marking particle ('then/so') linking the conditional to its consequence; все правило ('the whole rule') is the affected noun phrase, прахом is the instrumental case used adverbially meaning 'into dust/ashes', and пошло is the past neuter of пойти used metaphorically ('has gone' / 'went') to indicate that the rule has been ruined or destroyed.
segments.p1-7-g203: Но до того is an idiomatic degree expression: 'but to such an extent'. Человек (nominative) is the subject, пристрастен is the short-form adjective meaning 'is biased/partial' and takes the preposition к + dative (к системе, к отвлеченному выводу). Отвлеченному выводу is dative singular (к requires dative); отвлеченному is an adjective modifying вывод.
segments.p1-7-g204: Что introduces a result/explanatory clause ('that'). Готов is a predicate adjective meaning 'ready/willing' and is followed by the perfective infinitive исказить; умышленно is an adverb modifying исказить. Правду is the accusative direct object of исказить.
segments.p1-7-g205: This is a parallel idiomatic construction repeating готов + infinitive. Видом and слыхом are instrumental nouns used idiomatically ('by appearance', 'by hearing') with colloquial infinitives видать and слыхать; the negatives (не) precede the verbs, producing the sense 'to pretend not to see and not to hear.' The structure emphasizes deliberate refusal to notice by sight and hearing.
segments.p1-7-g206: Только restricts the motive ('merely/only'); чтоб introduces a purpose clause ('in order that' / 'so as to') followed by the infinitive оправдать. Свою is the reflexive possessive referring back to the subject ('his/her/one's own'), and логику is the accusative object of оправдать.
segments.p1-7-g207: Потому introduces a result/reason ('for that reason'/'that's why'). И here is a linking/emphatic particle attaching the reason to the action (colloquial 'and so/therefore I...'). Беру is 1st person singular present 'I take/use'; этот пример is the direct object in accusative ('this example').
segments.p1-7-g208: Что introduces the explanatory subordinate clause ('that/because'). Это is the demonstrative subject 'this/it'. Слишком is an adverb 'too/excessively' modifying the adjective яркий, which means 'striking/vivid' here; Russian omits the copula in the present, so the clause reads literally 'this is too vivid an example.'
segments.p1-7-g209: Да here has an optative/exhortative force ('do/just'), used with the imperative to urge the listener. Оглянитесь is the 2nd-person plural imperative 'look around' (reflexive form of оглядеться/оглянуться). Кругом is an adverb meaning 'around/everywhere' and completes the command.
segments.p1-7-g210: Кровь is the noun subject; рекою is an instrumental singular form used adverbially for comparison ('like a river' / 'in a river'), a somewhat poetic/graphic simile; льется is 3rd-person singular present of литься 'flows/is poured', so the clause means 'blood is flowing like a river.'
segments.p1-7-g211: Да ещё here functions as a connective 'and what's more' ('да' as conjunctive particle + ещё 'more/yet'). Развеселым (instrumental adjective) + таким образом is a standard pattern meaning 'in such a merry way'—the instrumental expresses manner ('in a merry manner'), with таким образом ('in such a manner') reinforcing it.
segments.p1-7-g212: Точно here is a comparative/simile marker meaning 'as if' or 'just like' (not exactness). Шампанское is the noun used as the target of the comparison ('like champagne'). Together they form the short comparative 'as if (it were) champagne.'
segments.p1-7-g213: Вот...вам is a colloquial demonstrative phrase—'Вот вам...' = 'here you have' or 'behold for you.' The structure is: Вот (draws attention) + dative вам (addresses the listener) + the noun phrase все наше девятнадцатое столетие ('the whole/entire nineteenth century').
segments.p1-7-g214: в котором = 'in which' is the relative pronoun phrase referring back to 'столетие'; жил is the past tense, masculine singular of жить ('lived') and appears before the subject for stylistic word order; и here functions as 'even/also' (emphatic), and Бокль is the name (placed after the verb).
segments.p1-7-g104: "Но в том-то и пагуба," is an emphatic set phrase: 'в том-то и' (в + demonstrative + particle -то + и) stresses that the thing just mentioned is exactly where the harm lies; 'пагуба' is a nominal predicate meaning 'ruin/misfortune'. There is no finite verb here because the clause is nominal and idiomatic.
segments.p1-7-g105: "что эта мудреная выгода ни в какую классификацию не попадает," — 'что' is the subordinate conjunction 'that'; 'эта мудреная выгода' is the demonstrative + adjective + noun ('this convoluted/overly clever advantage'); the negative correlative 'ни...не' frames 'в какую классификацию не попадает' meaning 'does not end up in any classification' (попадает is present imperfective, 'to end up/fall into'). Word order places the prepositional phrase before the verb, as is normal.
segments.p1-7-g106: "ни в один список не умещается." — again the negative 'ни...не' (emphatic 'not even into one') plus the prepositional phrase 'в один список' ('into a single list'); 'умещается' is the reflexive present of 'умещать', 'to fit; to be contained', third person singular, meaning it cannot be made to fit into any list.
segments.p1-7-g107: "У меня" is the standard possession construction with the preposition у + genitive and literally means 'at me', i.e. 'I have'; the comma after it simply sets off the parenthetical 'например'.
segments.p1-7-g108: "например," is an adverb meaning 'for example' or 'for instance' and here marks the remark as illustrative.
segments.p1-7-g109: "есть приятель." — 'есть' is the present of 'to be' used existentially in the 'у меня есть X' pattern to assert possession/existence; 'приятель' is the nom. predicate meaning 'a friend' or 'an acquaintance'.
segments.p1-7-g112: Эх is an interjection expressing a sigh, exclamation or emotional address (like 'ah' or 'oh'). Here it stands alone as a expressive opener and does not affect grammar beyond tone.
segments.p1-7-g113: Господа is the vocative/plural form of господин used to address several people ('gentlemen' or 'sirs'). It functions as the addressee in this exclamation and carries no verb.
segments.p1-7-g114: Да here is a colloquial particle introducing an emphatic comment (not a simple 'yes'); ведь is an emphasizer meaning 'after all'; и adds 'also' or 'too'. The phrase presents a predicate nominal (он приятель) with vam (dative) placed before the subject for emphasis: 'to you he is a friend.' Russian omits the copula in the present tense.
segments.p1-7-g115: Да и is a colloquial tag meaning something like 'and besides' or 'indeed'; кому here (dative of кто) begins a rhetorical question ('to whom' / 'who'). This short segment serves as the lead-in for the exclamatory question that follows.
segments.p1-7-g116: This is an exclamatory clause where the dative кому ('to whom') is fronted for force: 'to whom is he not a friend!' Он is the subject, приятель is a predicate noun, and не negates the predicate. Again, the present copula is omitted in Russian.
segments.p1-7-g60: Как starts the question as an interrogative adverb meaning 'how' or idiomatically 'what' in 'What do you think'; вы is the 2nd-person plural (or polite singular) subject; думаете is present-tense, 2nd-person plural indicative of думать, here a routine/questioning 'do you think'. The word order (question-word + subject + verb) is a normal Russian question pattern.
segments.p1-7-g61: бывает is the present form of бывать meaning 'occur' or 'happen' (habitual/occasional occurrence); ли is the enclitic particle that turns the clause into a yes/no question ('whether/does'); такой modifies случай meaning 'such a'; случай is the noun 'case/instance'. Together: 'does such a case happen/occur?'
segments.p1-7-g62: Вы is the 2nd-person plural (or formal) pronoun; смеетесь is the 2nd-person plural present indicative of смеяться ('laugh' or 'are laughing'), stating the fact that they are laughing. The semicolon gives an ironic or emphatic pause before the imperative that follows.
segments.p1-7-g63: смейтесь is the 2nd-person plural imperative of смеяться, an exhortation or ironic command 'laugh!' or 'mock!'. The comma after it and the following vocative mark the address to the audience.
segments.p1-7-g64: господа is the vocative/plural form of господин, used here to address the listeners as 'gentlemen' or 'sirs'; set off by commas as a direct address.
segments.p1-7-g65: но is a contrastive conjunction 'but/however'; только here means 'only' or 'just' ('but just/only answer'); отвечайте is the 2nd-person plural imperative of отвечать, commanding them to respond. The phrase functions as a request/command countering the laughter.
segments.p1-7-g66: совершенно is an adverb 'completely/entirely'; ли is the question particle ('whether/indeed'); верно is an adverb meaning 'correctly/truly'; сосчитаны is a short-form passive past participle (plural) of сосчитать, 'counted' or 'reckoned', agreeing with выгоды; выгоды человеческие is the noun phrase 'human advantages' with the adjective человеческие following the noun in a slightly marked/poetic order. The whole clause asks if the human advantages have been calculated entirely correctly.
segments.p1-7-g67: Нет ли is an existential rhetorical question meaning 'is there not' or 'are there not'; нет here expresses non-existence ('there is no/are there none') and ли makes it interrogative; таких is the plural genitive/accusative form of такой used after нет to mean 'such (ones)'.
segments.p1-7-g68: которые is the relative pronoun 'who/which/that' introducing a subordinate clause about those people; не только ... is the correlative 'not only ...' (только here = 'only/merely'); не ulожились is the negated past tense plural reflexive verb уложиться meaning 'fit in' or 'be fitted into' (here 'did not fit into the scheme'). The clause says they 'not only did not fit.'
segments.p1-7-g69: но и is the continuation 'but also/even'; не negates могут (can/are able to); могут is present tense, 3rd-person plural of мочь 'can/be able'; уложиться is the infinitive 'to fit (in)'; ни в какую is a fixed negative intensifier meaning 'into no ... whatsoever' (ни = 'not even', в какую = 'into any/which'); классификацию is the object 'classification'. Together: 'but also cannot be made to fit into any classification.'
segments.p1-7-g70: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning roughly 'after all' or 'you see,' used to introduce or justify the speaker's point; вы is the 2nd-person plural (or polite singular) pronoun 'you'. The phrase signals the speaker's appeal to the listeners' position/behavior.
segments.p1-7-g71: господа is a vocative plural addressing the audience as 'gentlemen' or 'sirs', repeating the direct address and adding formality/irony.
segments.p1-7-g72: сколько мне известно is an idiomatic parenthetical meaning 'as far as I know' or 'to my knowledge'; сколько functions adverbially here, мне is the dative 'to me', and известно is the impersonal predicate 'is known' (literally 'how much is known to me').
segments.p1-7-g73: весь ваш реестр человеческих выгод is the direct object 'your entire register/catalogue of human advantages' (весь modifies реестр; человеческих modifies выгоды, with the adjective following the noun in a slightly marked order). взяли is the past-tense plural of взять 'took' or 'adopted' (here 'adopted/took as'); средним числом is an instrumental phrase 'by an average number' or 'as an average figure' describing how they treated the register; из ... цифр и из ... формул marks the sources 'from statistical figures and from scientific-economic formulas'. The verb взяли agrees with the implied plural subject 'you'.
segments.p1-7-g187: Да-с is an emphatic interjection: да = 'yes' and the attached -с is a colloquial particle adding force or irony (not the preposition 'с'). It stands alone as an exclamation and prepares the bitter/ironic tone of the next word.
segments.p1-7-g188: Логистика is a noun used here as an exclamation — the speaker names the abstract system ('logistics' / systematic calculation) with irony. The word functions as the sentence's main noun and carries the dismissive or sarcastic thrust.
segments.p1-7-g189: This is an infinitival clause introduced by ведь ('after all') with утврeждать in the infinitive meaning 'to assert' or 'to claim.' Хоть here means 'even' (concessive) modifying эту теорию 'this theory.' Теорию обновления is 'the theory of renewal' (обновления is a genitive complement to теория). Всего рода человеческого is a genitive phrase 'of the whole human race' (всего = 'whole,' рода = 'race/kind,' человеческого = 'human'). Пoсредством governs the following genitive phrase система (системы) — 'by means of a system' — and его собственных выгод = 'of its own advantages/interests,' where его refers back to рода (the race) and собственных agrees with выгoд.
segments.p1-7-g190: Ведь это repeats the discourse marker 'after all' and then the demonstrative это ('this/it') which points back to the whole infinitival idea just stated; the phrase frames the speaker's summary/judgment of the preceding claim.
segments.p1-7-g191: По-моему is a fixed dative expression meaning 'in my opinion' (по + dative possessive form). Grammatically it's an adverbial qualifier attached to the following predicate.
segments.p1-7-g192: Почти means 'almost.' То же is the phrase 'the same (thing),' where то is the demonstrative 'that/it' and же is an emphatic particle reinforcing identity: together they mean 'almost the same.'
segments.p1-7-g195: Ну is an introductory particle ('well'); хоть here means 'at least'/'even' and introduces an example or concession; утверждать is an infinitive 'to assert' and names the action being mentioned. Together they mean 'well, to assert...'.
segments.p1-7-g196: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' and marks that what follows is an illustrative instance of the action named in the previous infinitive.
segments.p1-7-g197: Вслед за is a fixed prepositional sequence meaning 'following' or 'after' someone; it is followed by a noun in the instrumental (here the name Бокль in instrumental case). Grammatically this indicates that the assertion is being made 'in the wake of' or 'following' that author's opinion.
segments.p1-7-g198: Что introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'). От with the genitive (цивилизации) marks cause or source ('from/because of civilization'). Человек is a generic subject ('man' / humankind) and смягчается is a reflexive/passive-form present tense verb meaning 'is softened' or 'becomes softer.'
segments.p1-7-g199: Следственно is an adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore' and links the preceding claim to the result clause that follows.
segments.p1-7-g200: Становится is present-tense 'becomes' (3rd person singular). Менее is the comparative particle 'less' modifying the short-form adjective кровожаден ('bloodthirsty') used predicatively; и links the two coordinated comparative predicates, the second being 'менее способен к войне'—'less capable of war' where способен ('capable') takes the prepositional phrase к + dative (войне).
segments.p1-7-g201: По + dative (логике) here means 'according to' or 'by' a principle; the dative логике expresses the domain ('according to logic'). The enclitic particle -то on логике is a colloquial contrastive/emphatic particle (draws attention to 'logic' as opposed to something else) and the comma marks a small pause.
segments.p1-7-g202: Кажется is an impersonal present indicative meaning 'it seems' or 'apparently'. У него is the prepositional/possessive construction used idiomatically to mean 'in his case' or 'for him' (у + genitive). И так is a colloquial combination where и is an emphatic/augmentative particle ('even/also') and так means 'that way'; выходит is 3rd person singular present 'turns out/results' and ties the clause together: 'it seems that, for him, it turns out that way.'
segments.p1-7-g215: Вот вам is a short demonstrative/exclamatory phrase meaning “here you have” with вам in the dative (literally ‘to you’), used to present or point out something. Наполеон is nominative (the thing being presented); the dash marks an abrupt apposition or pause before a predicate. и introduces the first half of a correlative pair (“both … and …”), and великий is an adjectival predicate in nominative agreeing with Наполеон (Russian omits the copular verb 'is').
segments.p1-7-g216: The second и completes the correlative construction и … и … meaning “both … and …”; теперешний is another predicative adjective in the nominative, ‘present/contemporary,’ again with the copula omitted and referring back to Наполеон.
segments.p1-7-g217: Вот вам again presents something to the reader (вам = dative). Северная Америка is a proper name formed by adjective + noun (both in nominative) and is linked by a dash to a nominal predicate вековечный союз; the dash replaces a copula and creates an appositive/defining statement (‘is an everlasting union’).
segments.p1-7-g218: Вот вам is the same presenting phrase (‘here you have’); the comma marks a pause before the parenthetical adverb that follows.
segments.p1-7-g219: наконец is a parenthetical adverb meaning ‘finally’ or ‘at last,’ inserted to signal the last example in a list; it stands apart by commas and modifies the whole clause.
segments.p1-7-g220: карикатурный modifies the proper name that follows and is in adjective + proper-name order (nominative); the phrase functions as a nominal phrase predicate or appositive (‘a caricatured Schleswig–Holstein’), with the adjective coloring the place-name.
segments.p1-7-g223: И is a connective particle (‘and’) linking this question to the preceding remarks. что такое is a colloquial interrogative formula meaning ‘what is it that…’ (что = what; такое = such/a thing of that sort, used to make the question more pointed). смягчает is present-tense, 3rd-person singular, imperfective indicative ‘softens/mitigates’; в нас (‘in us’) marks the location/affected party with the preposition в + accusative (нас); цивилизация is the nominative subject performing the action.
segments.p1-7-g24: "да когда же," is a colloquial, rhetorical opener. "да" is a particle here adding emphasis or contrast (roughly "but/then"), "когда" is the interrogative "when," and "же" is an emphatic particle that strengthens the rhetorical question.
segments.p1-7-g25: "во-первых" is a frozen adverbial phrase meaning "first of all"; grammatically this is an ordering adverb made from the preposition variant "во" + ordinal "первых."
segments.p1-7-g26: "бывало" is an impersonal/habitual past adverb meaning "it used to happen" or "has it ever happened"; here it marks a general, repeated or proverbial occurrence and helps form the rhetorical question.
segments.p1-7-g27: "во все эти тысячелетия" is a temporal phrase: "во все" (throughout/all) + demonstrative "эти" + "тысячелетия" (plural). It means "throughout all these millennia/ages."
segments.p1-7-g28: "чтоб человек действовал только из одной своей собственной выгоды?" is the subordinate clause introduced by colloquial "чтоб" (short for "чтобы") meaning "that"/"that it be the case"; "человек" is the general noun "a person/man"; "действовал" is past-tense, imperfective (habitual/general) meaning "acted"; "только из одной своей собственной выгоды" is a prepositional phrase with "из" (="out of/from") indicating motive, and the stack "одной своей собственной" intensifies possession: "solely from his own, personal advantage."
segments.p1-7-g29: "Что же делать с миллионами фактов," is a rhetorical question: "Что...делать" uses the infinitive "делать" in a 'what to do' question; "же" adds emphasis; "с" governs the noun in the instrumental ("миллионами фактов") meaning "with millions of facts."
segments.p1-7-g30: "свидетельствующих о том," is a participial clause modifying "фактов": "свидетельствующих" is the present active participle (genitive plural) meaning "attesting" or "testifying," and "о том" is the fixed phrase "about the fact that."
segments.p1-7-g31: "как люди зазнамо," begins a clause headed by "как" (= "how/as"). "люди" is the general plural "people," and "зазнамо" is an archaic/adverbial word meaning acting knowingly or deliberately (in older usage often with an overtones of knowing arrogance); it modifies the manner in which people acted.
segments.p1-7-g32: "то есть вполне понимая свои настоящие выгоды," is an explanatory parenthesis: "то есть" = "that is," "вполне понимая" is a present participle construction "fully understanding," and "свои настоящие выгоды" is "their real/actual advantages," with the possessive "свои" referring back to "люди."
segments.p1-7-g33: "отставляли их на второй план и бросались на другую дорогу," describes the main actions: "отставляли" (past plural imperfective) = "put aside/left behind," "их" = "them" (the advantages), "на второй план" = "to a second place/into the background," and "и бросались" = "and flung themselves" (imperfective, reflexive), "на другую дорогу" = "onto another road/path."
segments.p1-7-g34: "на риск," is a prepositional phrase with "на" + accusative meaning "onto/into risk," indicating the type of path taken.
segments.p1-7-g35: "на авось," is an idiomatic phrase: "на" + "авось" (here meaning reliance on luck/chance) — "on a whim/hope/just hoping for the best."
segments.p1-7-g36: "никем и ничем не принуждаемые к тому," is a participial phrase: "никем и ничем" (by no one and nothing; instrumental) stresses absence of external compulsion, "не принуждаемые" is the present passive participle "not being forced/compelled," and "к тому" (="to that") refers back to the action just described.
segments.p1-7-g37: "а как будто именно только не желая указанной дороги," is a contrastive clause: "а" = "but/while," "как будто" = "as if," "именно только" intensifies ("precisely just"), "не желая" is a gerund/participle meaning "not wanting," and "указанной дороги" (genitive) = "the road pointed out/suggested." The whole phrase says: "but as if precisely because they did not want the indicated road."
segments.p1-7-g38: "и упрямо," is short and adverbial: "и" = "and/also," "упрямо" = "stubbornly," modifying the following verb phrase.
segments.p1-7-g39: "своевольно пробивали другую," continues the description: "своевольно" = "willfully/by their own will," "пробивали" (past imperfective) = "(they) forced/broke/forged (a way) through" by persistence, and "другую" = "another (one)."
segments.p1-7-g40: "трудную," is an adjective in the feminine accusative agreeing with the implied noun "дорогу": "difficult/hard."
segments.p1-7-g41: "нелепую," is another agreeing adjective: "absurd/incongruous," adding a quality to the road they forced through.
segments.p1-7-g42: "отыскивая ее чуть не в потемках." is a participial clause describing manner: "отыскивая" = present participle "searching out/looking for," "ее" = "it/her" (the road), "чуть не" = idiom "almost/nearly," and "в потемках" = "in the dark/in obscurity." Together: "searching for it almost in the dark."
segments.p1-7-g43: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning 'after all' or 'you know' used to recall or reinforce what was just said; it doesn't change the sentence structure and is set off by a comma here.
segments.p1-7-g44: Значит is another parenthetical particle meaning 'then/so/it means' that signals an inference or conclusion; it functions adverbially and does not act as a main verb.
segments.p1-7-g45: This whole clause contains the dative experiencer им ('to them'), the intensifier действительно ('indeed/really'), the demonstrative это ('this') modifying the coordinated subject упрямство и своеволие ('stubbornness and willfulness'), the past linking verb было ('was'), the comparative predicate приятнее ('more pleasant'), and the genitive phrase всякой выгоды ('than any advantage') expressing the thing compared; note that приятно/приятнее is used predicatively with было to form the past comparative.
segments.p1-7-g48: A single-word exclamation: the noun выгода ('advantage/profit') is used rhetorically as an interjection to echo or mock the idea of 'advantage' just discussed.
segments.p1-7-g1: The clause is a simple declarative sentence. Но is an adversative conjunction ('but'); все это ('all this') is the nominative subject phrase; золотые мечты is a nominal predicate in the plural (Russian omits the present-tense copula 'is'). The adjective золотые is literal 'golden' used figuratively to mean fanciful/ideal, and мечты is the plural noun 'dreams/aspirations.'
segments.p1-7-g2: О is an exclamatory interjection equivalent to 'Oh' used to introduce an address or an emotional appeal to the listener/reader.
segments.p1-7-g3: Скажите is the imperative form (polite/plural) of сказать: here it functions as a request or challenge 'tell me' / 'do tell'. It addresses the audience and introduces the rhetorical question that follows.
segments.p1-7-g4: Кто это первый объявил is an interrogative subordinate clause: кто 'who'; это is a discourse/emphatic particle 'this/it'; первый 'first' (ordinal); объявил is past tense, perfective, masculine singular 'announced/declared'. Together: 'who first announced this.'
segments.p1-7-g5: Кто первый провозгласил repeats the previous structure for rhetorical emphasis. Провозгласил is also past tense, perfective, stronger than объявил — 'proclaimed' or 'declared emphatically.'
segments.p1-7-g6: Что человек потому только делает пакости introduces the reported thesis: что 'that'; человек used generically 'a man'/'man/people'; потому только is a collocation meaning 'only for that reason' or 'only because of that' (потому modifying the cause, with только restricting it); делает is present tense imperfective 'does' (habitual/ongoing); пакости is plural accusative 'nasty deeds/mischief.'
segments.p1-7-g7: Что не знает настоящих своих интересов continues the reported clause: что 'that'; не знает is present negative 'does not know/ is unaware of'; настоящих (genitive plural) 'real/genuine' modifies интересов; своих is the reflexive possessive 'his/one's own' referring back to человек; интересов is genitive plural 'interests/concerns' governed by знать.
segments.p1-7-g8: А что если б его просветить begins a counterfactual hypothetical: а adds 'but/and' to shift the argument; что если б is the colloquial conditional 'what if' (если with the conditional particle б = бы); его is the accusative pronoun 'him'; просветить is the perfective infinitive 'to enlighten' (i.e. to make aware).
segments.p1-7-g9: Открыть ему глаза на его настоящие is a continuation of the hypothetical result: открыть (perfective infinitive) 'to open/reveal'; ему is dative 'to him'; глаза is the literal 'eyes' used metaphorically 'open his eyes'; на + его настоящие (adjective left hanging here) introduces the object phrase 'to his real ...' (the noun follows in the next segment).
segments.p1-7-g10: Нормальные интересы completes the object of открыть глаза на: нормальные 'normal/regular' modifies интересы 'interests' (nominative/accusative plural), so the full idea is 'open his eyes to his real, normal interests.'
segments.p1-7-g11: То человек тотчас же перестал бы делать пакости is the conditional result clause: то is a linking particle ('then'/'so'); человек is the subject; тотчас же 'immediately/at once' (adverbial); перестал бы is past perfective + conditional particle бы meaning 'would cease/stop'; делать пакости uses the imperfective делать 'do' with пакости 'nasty deeds' — 'would immediately stop doing nasty things.'
segments.p1-7-g12: Тотчас же стал бы добрым и благородным gives the parallel positive result: тотчас же 'at once'; стал бы is past + conditional particle 'would become'; добрым and благородным are predicative adjectives in the instrumental case (typical with стать) meaning 'would become kind and noble.'
segments.p1-7-g13: Потому что introduces the explanation clause: because/for the reason that. It signals the following causal subordinate clause.
segments.p1-7-g14: Будучи просвещенным и понимая настоящие свои выгоды is a circumstantial participial phrase giving the state and simultaneous action: будучи ('being', a gerund from быть) + просвещенным ('enlightened', participial/adjectival form) expresses 'being enlightened'; понимая is a present adverbial participle 'and understanding/realizing'; настоящие свои выгоды is the direct object 'his real interests/advantages' (свои refers back to 'человек').
segments.p1-7-g15: Именно увидел бы в добре собственную свою выгоду states the expected recognition: именно 'precisely/indeed' (emphatic); увидел бы is perfective past + conditional 'would see/realize' (увидеть can mean 'notice' or 'realize' here); в добре is an archaic locative-like construction 'in goodness' ('in the good'); собственную свою (double possessive) is emphatic 'his very own'; выгоду is accusative 'advantage/benefit.'
segments.p1-7-g16: А известно is an impersonal comment linking the general truth: а 'and/but' here connects the remark; известно is impersonal 'it is known' / 'as is known' (functions like 'one knows' or 'it is obvious').
segments.p1-7-g17: Что ни один человек не может действовать зазнамо против собственных своих выгод is the asserted general principle: что 'that'; ни один человек = 'no single person' (negative universal using ни + один); не может действовать = 'cannot act' (modal cannot); зазнамо is an adverb meaning 'knowingly' / 'arrogantly' (here 'knowingly' — i.e. deliberately); против governs the genitive собственных своих выгод 'against his own interests' (double possessive for emphasis).
segments.p1-7-g18: Следственно is a linking adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore,' introducing the inference drawn from the previous 'it is known' statement.
segments.p1-7-g19: Так сказать is the fixed idiom 'so to speak' or 'as it were' used to soften or frame the following generalization; так 'so/thus' + сказать 'to say/mean' form the set phrase.
segments.p1-7-g20: По необходимости стал бы делать добро? is the concluding rhetorical question: по необходимости 'by necessity' / 'necessarily'; стал бы here is the conditional 'would come to / would begin' (стать + бы); делать добро is the verb phrase 'do good' (делать + добро = perform good deeds). The whole asks whether, therefore, he would necessarily start doing good.
segments.p1-7-g21: О is the vocative/exclamatory particle 'O' (addressing someone) and младенец is a poetic nominative used as a vocative-like address 'infant'/'baby' — an apostrophic exclamation expressing astonishment or mock compassion.
segments.p1-7-g22: О is the exclamatory particle 'O'; чистое is the neuter nominative (agreeing with дитя) meaning 'pure' or 'clean' used as an epithet addressing the child.
segments.p1-7-g23: Невинное дитя is the second epithet + noun: невинное 'innocent' (neuter to agree with дитя); дитя is the neuter noun 'child.' The phrase is an apostrophic address ('O pure, innocent child!').
segments.p1-7-g224: The verb выработывает is present tense, 3rd person singular (imperfective) with the subject Цивилизация; it means 'produces/develops.' The phrase в человеке is a locative expression placed after the verb ('produces in a person'). Только restricts what is produced to the following noun phrase; многосторонность takes ощущений in the genitive plural as its complement ('a multiplicity of sensations'). The final и is a trailing conjunction (ellipsis) signaling continuation into the next clause.
segments.p1-7-g227: Решительно is an adverbial intensifier here ('absolutely/decisively') modifying the negative phrase ничего больше. Ничего is the negative pronoun 'nothing,' and больше functions as an adverb meaning 'more/no more' after a negative (ничего больше = 'nothing more/anything else'). Together they form a short, emphatic sentence fragment closing the thought begun by the preceding и.
segments.p1-7-g228: А is a coordinating particle here ('and' / transition). Через + noun (развитие) means 'through/by the development of ...' and эта(more fully этой многосторонности) is a genitive attribute 'of that multiplicity.' Человек is the subject ('a man' / 'a person'), and ещё here stretches the sense to 'still' / 'even further' before the following modalizing words.
segments.p1-7-g229: Пожалуй is a parenthetical particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be,' softening the assertion that follows.
segments.p1-7-g230: Дойдет is future tense, 3rd person singular of дойти (perfective) and here forms the idiom дойти до того, 'reach the point that.' До governs того (demonstrative genitive), linking to the subordinate clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-7-g231: Что introduces the subordinate clause ('that ...'). Отыщет is future tense, 3rd person singular of the perfective отыскать 'to find,' used here as a real/future possibility. В крови is the prepositional expression 'in blood' (used idiomatically to mean finding enjoyment in bloodshed/violence), and наслаждение is the direct object 'pleasure.'
segments.p1-7-g232: Ведь is a discourse particle 'after all' that signals a reminder or reinforcement. Это is the demonstrative 'this/that,' and уж is an adverb meaning 'already' that strengthens the past reference. Случалось is the past imperfective (случаться) meaning 'would happen/has happened' — imperfective here suggests it happened on occasion or at least once in the past. С ним uses the instrumental 'with him,' a standard Russian construction meaning 'happened to him.'
segments.p1-7-g163: A short interjection used to start or prompt speech; here it functions as a discourse marker meaning “well.” It’s an informal colloquial opening before the quoted fragment.
segments.p1-7-g164: A compact colloquial phrase: так (so/then) + все-таки (after all) + выгоды (the noun 'advantages/benefits' in the bare nominal form) + же (emphatic particle). Grammatically this is an elliptical nominal exclamation (no verb) reporting what the interrupter says; 'все-таки' expresses concession/insistence ('after all').
segments.p1-7-g165: Finite verb перебиваете (2nd person plural present) + subject pronoun вы (explicit for emphasis/formality) + object меня (accusative). The verb is present progressive in Russian usage (you interrupt me / are interrupting me).
segments.p1-7-g166: Позвольте is the polite imperative (2nd person plural) meaning 'allow me' / 'permit me.' The added enclitic -с (older colloquial flavor) softens or colors the expression; here it marks a slightly old-fashioned or jocular politeness.
segments.p1-7-g167: Мы (we) + ещё (still/yet) + объяснимся (future 1st person plural of объясниться, reflexive), meaning 'we will explain ourselves' or 'we’ll make ourselves understood.' The reflexive verb indicates the speaker will clarify or account for themselves.
segments.p1-7-g168: Да и (and besides / moreover) introduces a qualification; не inverts the sense ('not in a pun') and в каламбуре дело is a prepositional phrase meaning 'the matter isn't in a pun.' The whole denies that the point is a mere pun.
segments.p1-7-g169: А contrasts what follows with the previous clause; в том is a locative/demonstrative phrase 'in that' used to point to the real issue which will be explained in the subordinate clause.
segments.p1-7-g170: Subordinating conjunction что ('that') introduces the explanation: эта выгода (this advantage) is the subject, and the predicate follows. Именно тем (literally 'exactly by that') is an emphatic phrase indicating the reason or quality by which the advantage is remarkable (замечательна). Russian allows the predicative adjective замечательна without an explicit copula.
segments.p1-7-g171: Continuation of the subordinate clause: что introduces a second 'that' clause where всё наши классификации is a direct object phrase and разрушает is the verb (3rd person singular to agree with 'эта выгода'). И links a coordinate object (все системы) — both 'all our classifications' and 'all systems' are what the advantage destroys.
segments.p1-7-g172: A participial phrase modifying 'системы': составленные is the past passive participle 'composed/devised' and любителями (instrumental) names the agent ('by amateurs/enthusiasts') of рода человеческого ('of the human race'). Для счастья ('for the happiness of') is a purpose phrase modifying the systems. The repetition рода человеческого repeats the genitive 'of mankind.'
segments.p1-7-g173: Постоянно (constantly) is an adverb modifying the verb разбивает (3rd person singular), which here means 'breaks into pieces / shatters.' This repeats and intensifies the earlier verb разрушает, stressing continuous destructive action.
segments.p1-7-g174: Одним словом is a fixed idiom meaning 'in a word' or 'to sum up' (instrumental одим + слово). It introduces a brief summary or conclusion of what was just said.
segments.p1-7-g175: Всему is the dative singular of весь used here to mean 'to everything' (the thing being affected), and мешает is a 3rd person singular present verb 'hinders'/'prevents.' Russian verbs of hindrance typically take the dative object.
segments.p1-7-g176: Но прежде чем combines a coordinating particle 'but' with the subordinating phrase 'прежде чем' ('before') to introduce a temporal clause. Я вам назову is a simple future clause ('I will name/tell you'), where вам is the dative indirect object and эту выгоду is the accusative direct object.
segments.p1-7-g177: Я хочу + infinitive компрометировать себя gives 'I want to compromise myself' (себя is the reflexive/accusative object). Лично ('personally') qualifies the action. И потому дерзко объявляю is coordinate: 'and therefore I boldly declare' — объявляю is present-tense performative ('I declare').
segments.p1-7-g178: Что introduces the content clause (that ...). Все эти прекрасные системы is the subject noun phrase ('all these splendid systems') of the clause to follow; the clause will predicate something about them.
segments.p1-7-g179: A further nominal group: все эти теории разъяснения человечеству настоящих — 'all these theories of explaining to humanity the real...' Here разъяснения is a noun 'explanation' (genitive) used in an attributive/genitive chain with человечеству (dative: 'to humanity') and the adjective настоящих (genitive plural 'real') which connects to the following noun 'interests.'
segments.p1-7-g180: Нормальных его интересов places the adjective нормальных (genitive plural) before the possessive его and the noun интересов (genitive plural) — together 'its/ their normal interests.' The genitive continues the chain governed by 'теории разъяснения.' С тем begins a purposive construction ('with the aim/intent') that will be completed by the following 'чтоб' clause.
segments.p1-7-g181: Чтоб is the colloquial form of чтобы ('in order that/so that') introducing a purpose/result clause; оно is the dummy subject 'it' (referring back to человечеству/humanity as the grammatical subject of the intended result).
segments.p1-7-g182: Необходимо (necessarily/inevitably) modifies the adverbial participle phrase стремясь достигнуть (gerund + infinitive: 'striving to attain'), where стремясь is the деепричастие (adverbial participle) indicating concurrent action and достигнуть is the perfective infinitive 'to reach/attain.' The phrase specifies the manner or means by which 'it' is expected to change.
segments.p1-7-g183: Стало бы is the past/stative form of стать plus the conditional particle бы → 'would become.' Оно (neuter) is understood from the earlier clause. Тотчас же (at once/immediately) places temporal emphasis; добрым и благородным are predicate adjectives describing the resulting state.
segments.p1-7-g184: Покамест is an adverb meaning 'for the time being' or 'for now,' qualifying the speaker's following evaluative remark; it is parenthetical here.
segments.p1-7-g185: По моему мненью is an idiomatic older-style dative expression meaning 'in my opinion' (по + dative). Note the archaic spelling мненью (dative of мнение); the whole phrase qualifies the assertion that follows.
segments.p1-7-g186: Одна логистика is a pointed summative predicate: одна ('mere/just/only') + логистика ('logistics'). The speaker dismisses the lofty theories/systems as nothing more than 'logistics' — practical, technical planning — rather than moral reforms.
segments.p1-7-g324: A discourse particle placed at the start of the sentence meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', marking the speaker's concession or acknowledgement before he qualifies it.
segments.p1-7-g325: An impersonal modal construction: 'никак нельзя' = 'in no way is it possible' + infinitive 'гарантировать' ('to guarantee'). The parenthetical '(это уж я теперь говорю)' is a comment by the narrator: 'this, mind you, I say now,' where 'уж' is an emphatic particle and 'говорю' is 1st-person present.
segments.p1-7-g326: A subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction 'что' ('that') with temporal/conditional adverb 'тогда' ('then/in that case') and the future negative 'не будет' ('there will not be' / 'it won't be'). It states the thing that cannot be guaranteed.
segments.p1-7-g327: A linking adverb 'например' ('for example') introducing an illustrative possibility of what 'won't be guaranteed.'
segments.p1-7-g328: 'Ужасно скучно' is a predicative phrase: 'ужасно' is an intensive adverb ('extremely/terribly') modifying the predicative adjective/adverb 'скучно' ('boring/tedious'). The parenthetical causal clause begins with 'потому что' ('because'), then the colloquial exclamation 'что ж' ('what then/what on earth'), followed by 'и делать-то' where 'то' is the emphatic particle attached to the infinitive phrase 'делать' ('to do'), giving 'what is there to do (then)?'.
segments.p1-7-g329: A temporal subordinate clause 'когда все будет расчислено по табличке' — 'when everything will have been calculated/figured out according to the chart.' 'Будет расчислено' is a future passive/perfective periphrasis (neuter short form), and 'по табличке' means 'according to the table/chart.'
segments.p1-7-g330: 'Зато' is a contrasting particle meaning 'but on the other hand' or 'at least.' The clause 'все будет чрезвычайно благоразумно' is a simple future assertion: 'everything will be extraordinarily prudent/sensible,' with 'чрезвычайно' as an adverb of degree modifying the predicate 'благоразумно' (used predicatively here).
segments.p1-7-g331: Конечно is a sentence‑initial discourse adverb meaning “of course” or “naturally,” used here to mark the speaker’s aside or resignation; it does not change form to agree with anything.
segments.p1-7-g332: От скуки is a preposition + genitive phrase meaning “out of/from boredom.” Чего не выдумаешь is an exclamatory idiom: чего is an exclamatory/indefinite particle (colloquial “what/just think of it”), не is the negation particle, and выдумаешь is 2nd person singular of the perfective verb выдумать (so formally future tense) used generically/exclamatorily to mean “one/you can invent” or “people invent.”
segments.p1-7-g333: Ведь is a particle meaning “after all” (gives explanatory force). И here has the emphatic sense “even/also.” Золотые булавки is a nominative plural noun phrase “golden pins.” От скуки again is “out of/from boredom.” Втыкаются is 3rd person plural present (or a present habitual) of the reflexive verb втыкаться (–ся gives an intransitive/passive‑like sense), meaning “are stuck into / get stuck in / get stuck (into things).”
segments.p1-7-g334: Но is the coordinating conjunction “but.” Это is a demonstrative pronoun “this/that” referring to the previous idea. Бы is the conditional/modal particle (postpositive here) producing “would.” Все modifies ничего; ничего here is a colloquial predicate meaning literally “nothing” but idiomatically “no matter / all right / not a problem,” so это бы всё ничего = “that would be all right/harmless.”
segments.p1-7-g335: Скверно functions as a predicative adverb meaning 'it is bad' or 'unpleasant'; то is a demonstrative pronoun that links to the forthcoming subordinate clause ('that ...'). The parenthetical «это опять-таки я говорю» interrupts the main clause: это = 'this/it', опять-таки is an emphatic 'again' (опять with the intensifier таки), and я говорю is present tense 'I say' marking the narrator's aside.
segments.p1-7-g336: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. Чего доброго is an idiomatic genitive phrase (чего = genitive of что, доброго = adjectival genitive) used as a hedge meaning 'for all one knows' / 'God knows what' — it softens the assertion and expresses a possible/unexpected outcome.
segments.p1-7-g337: Пожалуй is a modal/parenthetical adverb meaning 'perhaps' or 'probably' that further hedges the statement; here it qualifies the likelihood expressed by the previous idiom.
segments.p1-7-g338: И here functions as 'even' / 'also', adding emphasis to the unexpected recipient. Золотым булавкам is dative plural ('to the golden pins') and is the indirect object of обрадуются. Тогда is an adverb 'then' / 'in that case' modifying the verb. Обрадуются is 3rd-person plural of perfective обрадоваться — literally 'will be pleased' or 'will rejoice'; in this hedged context it conveys 'might even be pleased'.
segments.p1-7-g392: Человеку надо is an impersonal-dative construction: dative Человеку + надо means 'a person needs' or 'it is necessary for a person'. The dash marks a pause before the specified thing needed. Одного только самостоятельного хотенья is a genitive noun phrase (одного ... хотенья) dependent on надо (elliptically: 'needs one ... desire'); только = 'only/just' restricts одного, and самостоятельного agrees in case/gender/number with хотенья.
segments.p1-7-g393: чего бы эта самостоятельность ни стоила и к чему бы ни привела is a pair of concessive clauses introduced by interrogative pronouns in subordinate use (чего, к чему) plus the particle бы and the particle ни to form a 'no matter what/wherever' sense. Эта самостоятельность is the grammatical subject in the first clause; стоила and привела are past-tense forms used with бы to create subjunctive/conditional meaning ('might cost', 'might lead to'). И links the two coordinated concessive clauses.
segments.p1-7-g394: Ну и is a colloquial introductory/contrasting particle combination ('well, and/indeed') used to signal a resigned or emphatic comment. Хотенье is the nominative subject ('desire'); ведь is a conversational particle ('after all/you know') adding emphasis. Черт знает is an idiomatic predicate: черт ('the devil') is the nominal subject of знает (3rd person present 'knows'), but together they form the set phrase meaning 'the devil knows' or 'God only knows' (i.e., 'nobody really knows').
segments.p1-7-g310: Тогда-то is the adverb тогда with the enclitic particle -то for emphasis, giving 'then/just then' (pointing back to the moment or condition just mentioned). The particle tightens or stresses the temporal deixis.
segments.p1-7-g311: Это всё вы говорите is an emphatic clause: это (demonstrative 'this/that') foregrounds the idea, всё ('all') qualifies what follows, and вы говорите is 2nd person plural present indicative 'you say' — the word order stresses that it is you who say all this.
segments.p1-7-g312: Настанут is the 3rd person plural future of the perfective настать, 'will begin/arrive.' Новые (nom. pl.) modifies отношения (nom. pl.), so the whole NP is the subject: 'new economic relations will arise.'
segments.p1-7-g313: Совсем уж готовые и тоже вычисленные — two nominative plural participial/adjectival modifiers of 'отношения'. Совсем уж (colloquial intensifier) = 'completely/utterly'; уж is an emphatic particle. Вычисленные is a past passive participle ('calculated') agreeing in number and case; с + instrumental (математическою точностью) indicates the means 'with mathematical precision.' Note the archaic adjective ending -ою in математическою (old spelling/style).
segments.p1-7-g314: Так что introduces the result clause 'so that.' В один миг is a time adverbial 'in one instant.' Исчезнут is 3rd person plural future of исчезнуть, 'will disappear,' with всевозможные вопросы as the nominative subject ('every possible questions/issues').
segments.p1-7-g315: Собственно потому is a connective: собственно ('precisely/properly') intensifies потому ('for that reason'/'because'), preparing the causal explanation that follows.
segments.p1-7-g316: Что introduces the explanatory subordinate clause. На них ('on/to them') refers back to the вопросы; получатся is the 3rd person plural future of the perfective получиться, here passive/impersonal in sense 'will be produced/obtained' (i.e. 'answers will be obtained for them'), and всевозможные ответы is the nominative plural subject/thing produced.
segments.p1-7-g317: Выстроится is the 3rd person singular future (perfective reflexive выстроиться) meaning 'will be built/erected.' The verb precedes the noun in inversion for stylistic effect; хрустальный дворец is the nominative subject 'a crystal palace.'
segments.p1-7-g318: Тогда is a standalone temporal adverb 'then' used as a one-word sentence/segue; it repeats or emphasizes the time/condition just mentioned.
segments.p1-7-g321: Ну is an interjection/particle 'well' used to introduce or soften the following remark.
segments.p1-7-g322: Одним словом (literally 'with one word') is an idiom meaning 'in a word' or 'to sum up'; словом is in the instrumental as part of this set phrase.
segments.p1-7-g323: Тогда again 'then'; прилетит is 3rd person singular future of прилететь ('will arrive/fly in'), and птица Каган is a nominal phrase (subject) 'the bird Kagan' — word order is standard subject after verb, with a slightly dramatic rhythm.
segments.p1-7-g255: «— сами решите.» is an imperative/indicative address: «сами» is an emphatic/reflexive pronoun meaning 'yourselves' or 'by your own selves', placed before the verb for emphasis; «решите» is the 2nd person plural perfective/future form (or polite/plural imperative sense) of решить, here functioning as a command/suggestion 'decide'. The short clause is elliptical and directs the interlocutors to make their own decision.
segments.p1-7-g256: «Говорят,» is an impersonal present/future reportive construction meaning 'they say' or 'it is said' used to introduce an anecdote or reported fact; the verb is 3rd person plural present (or general reportive) and lacks an explicit subject.
segments.p1-7-g257: This whole clause contains a main clause with a parenthesis. «Клеопатра» is the subject; «(извините за пример из римской истории)» is a parenthetical apology/excuse for giving an example drawn from Roman history (извините = 'excuse me/please excuse'); «любила» is the imperfective past meaning 'was fond of / used to' and introduces the infinitival action «втыкать» ('to stick/drive in'); «золотые булавки» is the direct object in plural ('gold pins'); «в груди своих невольниц» marks where the pins were stuck ('into the breasts of her slaves'), with «своих» a reflexive possessive referring back to the subject (Cleopatra); «и находила наслаждение в их криках и корчах» is coordinate: находила (past imperfective 'found/experienced') наслаждение ('pleasure') governed by the preposition «в» + nouns «их криках и корчах» ('their cries and convulsions/grimaces').
segments.p1-7-g258: «Вы скажете,» uses the 2nd person plural future/perfective form сказáть → «скажете» to introduce an imagined objection or retort: 'you will say' / 'one might say'. It is formulaic here and frames the following clauses as what the reader/objector would assert.
segments.p1-7-g259: «что это было во времена,» begins a subordinate clause with the conjunction «что» ('that'); «это было» is a neutral past statement 'this/it was'; «во» is the prepositional variant of «в» used before certain words and here means 'in'; «времена» is plural 'times' or 'periods'—the phrase sets up 'in the times [that follow]'.
segments.p1-7-g260: «говоря относительно,» is a parenthetical gerund phrase: «говоря» ('speaking') + «относительно» ('relatively'/'with respect to') = 'relatively speaking' or 'so to speak' and modifies the preceding phrase, marking a qualification of the statement.
segments.p1-7-g261: «варварские;» is the predicate adjective (plural) 'barbaric' describing the previously mentioned 'times' (времена). The semicolon indicates a pause before the narrator restates or elaborates the point.
segments.p1-7-g262: «что и теперь времена варварские,» is another clause introduced by «что» ('that'): «и теперь» ('even now' / 'still now') emphasizes continuity; «времена варварские» repeats that 'the times are barbaric'—the adjective agrees in plural with 'times'.
segments.p1-7-g263: «потому что (тоже говоря относительно) и теперь булавки втыкаются;» gives the reason: «потому что» ('because'); the parenthetical «тоже говоря относительно» ('also, relatively speaking') hedges the claim; «и теперь» ('even now') again; «булавки втыкаются» is a present-form passive/implicit-reflexive construction ('pins are stuck [into people]') where втыкаться indicates being driven into someone — an impersonal/passive observation.
segments.p1-7-g264: «что и теперь человек хоть и научился иногда видеть яснее,» continues the list of arguments: «что» ('that'); «и теперь человек» ('even now man/people'); «хоть и» is a concessive set phrase 'although/even though'; «научился» is perfective past 'has learned (to)' with the infinitive «видеть» ('to see'); «иногда» ('sometimes'); «яснее» is the comparative 'more clearly' — 'although man has in some instances learned to see more clearly'.
segments.p1-7-g265: «чем во времена варварские,» completes the comparative: «чем» ('than'); «во времена варварские» ('in barbaric times') repeats the temporal comparison—standard comparative clause introduced by чем.
segments.p1-7-g266: «но еще далеко не приучился поступать так,» is the main counterpoint: «но» ('but'); «еще далеко не» is an idiomatic negation 'still far from' or 'by no means yet'; «приучился» (reflexive perfective past) means 'has been trained/accustomed'; «поступать» is the imperfective infinitive 'to act, to behave'; «так» ('that way') indicates the manner—together: 'but is still far from being accustomed to act that way.'
segments.p1-7-g267: «как ему разум и науки указывают.» is a comparative clause introduced by «как» ('as'/'the way that'); «ему» is dative 'to him'; «разум и науки» ('reason and the sciences/learning') is a compound subject that governs the plural verb; «указывают» is present plural 'point out' or 'indicate' — 'as reason and learning indicate to him.'
segments.p1-7-g268: Но всё-таки is a combined particle meaning 'but still/ nevertheless' (Но = 'but', всё-таки = 'after all/still'); вы совершенно уверены uses the short-form adjective уверены (plural) agreeing with вы, meaning 'you are completely sure.'
segments.p1-7-g269: что introduces a reported clause 'that ...'; он is the subject 'he'; непременно is an adverb 'certainly/without fail'; приучится is the reflexive perfective verb приучиться in the future third-person singular: 'will get used to / will be trained.'
segments.p1-7-g270: когда begins a temporal clause 'when/once'; совсем = 'completely/entirely'; пройдут is the third-person plural (future) of пройти, here meaning 'will pass/cease' and agrees with the plural subject 'привычки'; кой-какие is a colloquial two-part modifier meaning 'some/certain'; старые = 'old/former' modifies привычки.
segments.p1-7-g271: This continues the temporal structure with two coordinated 'when' clauses joined by и. дурные привычки = 'bad habits' (дурные modifies привычки). когда здравый смысл и наука — 'when common/sound sense and science' (здравый смысл is a fixed collocation meaning 'common sense'); вполне перевоспитают и нормально направят are future, third-person plural perfective verbs meaning 'will fully re-educate and will properly direct,' taking натуру человеческую ('human nature' in the accusative) as their object. Note the adjective человеческую follows натуру here for rhetorical emphasis.
segments.p1-7-g233: Замечали ли вы is a past‑tense question framed with the enclitic ли: замечали is the past imperfective plural (or polite past for вы) meaning 'did you/ have you noticed', ли marks it as a yes/no question, and вы is the 2nd‑person plural/formal subject 'you'.
segments.p1-7-g234: This subordinate clause begins with the conjunction что 'that'. самые утонченные кровопроливцы is a nominal phrase with самые + adjective (утончённые = 'refined') modifying кровопроливцы ('those who shed blood, perpetrators of bloodshed'); почти сплошь ('almost entirely') is an adverbial intensifier placed before the copula были ('were'), and самые цивилизованные господа is the predicate nominal with самые marking a superlative/emphatic 'the very most civilized gentlemen'.
segments.p1-7-g235: которым is a dative plural relative pronoun 'to whom', referring back to господа. все эти разные Атиллы да Стеньки Разины is the nominative plural subject of the verb in the relative clause; да is a colloquial/archaic conjunction here meaning 'and'. иной раз is an adverbial phrase 'at times / sometimes'. в подметки не годились is an idiom: в подметки 'to the soles [of their shoes]' + не годились (past plural of годиться) meaning 'were not worth / did not measure up'.
segments.p1-7-g236: и если они не так ярко бросаются в глаза is a conditional/adversative phrase: и если 'and if', они 'they' (referring to the civilized gentlemen), не так ярко 'not so vividly/brightly' modifying бросаются в глаза — a reflexive construction meaning 'to catch the eye / be conspicuous' (present tense, 3rd pl).
segments.p1-7-g237: как Атилла и Стенька Разин is a simple comparative/example: как = 'like/as', followed by the two names (Attila and Stenka Razin) used as striking, notorious examples.
segments.p1-7-g238: так это именно потому is the beginning of the result clause: так 'that being so / therefore', это 'this/it', именно 'precisely/exactly', потому 'for that reason' — together meaning 'that is precisely why...'.
segments.p1-7-g239: что они слишком часто встречаются is the subordinate clause giving the reason: что 'that', они 'they', слишком часто 'too often' (слишком as intensifier + часто 'often'), встречаются is present 3rd‑pl reflexive/intransitive 'are encountered / occur / are found' (i.e. are common).
segments.p1-7-g240: слишком обыкновенны is a short‑form predicative adjective plural: слишком 'too' + обыкновенны (short plural of обыкновенный) meaning 'too ordinary / commonplace'.
segments.p1-7-g241: примелькались is a perfective/reflexive past plural verb (примелькаться) meaning 'have become familiar / grown familiar to the point of being tiresome' — a single predicate summarizing the result.
segments.p1-7-g242: По крайней мере is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'at least' / 'at any rate'. По is a preposition used here as part of the idiom, крайней is the genitive of крайний 'extreme', and мере is the dative/genitive of мера 'measure' as part of the set phrase.
segments.p1-7-g243: от цивилизации человек стал если не более кровожаден is causal: от 'because of / from', цивилизации is genitive 'civilization'; человек 'man/people' is the subject; стал is the past of стать 'became'; если не более кровожаден is a concessive phrase 'if not more bloodthirsty' (если не = 'if not', более = 'more', кровожаден is short‑form adjective 'bloodthirsty').
segments.p1-7-g244: то уже is a connective: то 'then/so' (links the alternative just stated) and уже 'already' — it signals the second alternative in the contrast begun by если не... .
segments.p1-7-g245: наверно is an adverb meaning 'probably / presumably', a parenthetical hedge modifying the following evaluation.
segments.p1-7-g246: хуже is the comparative adjective 'worse', standing as the main predicative evaluation in the second alternative.
segments.p1-7-g247: гаже кровожаден uses the older comparative gaже (an emphatic comparative 'even worse / more vilely') modifying the short‑form adjective кровожаден 'bloodthirsty'; together it intensifies the moral condemnation: 'more vilely bloodthirsty'.
segments.p1-7-g248: чем прежде is the standard comparative phrase: чем 'than' and прежде 'before / formerly', completing the comparison 'worse ... than before.'
segments.p1-7-g249: This whole clause narrates a past habit: Прежде 'before' / formerly; он 'he' (generic or particular man); видел 'saw/perceived' (past tense) takes the prepositional phrase в кровопролитии 'in bloodshed' as the context and справедливость 'justice' as the object of perception. и с покойною совестью is an instrumental phrase 'with a calm conscience' (покойною is an older/dialectal instrumental form), and истреблял is the imperfective past 'exterminated' (habitual/ongoing in the past) with кого следовало 'whomever it was necessary/right to' — where следовало is used impersonally/elliptically to mean 'ought to be'.
segments.p1-7-g250: теперь же мы хоть и считаем кровопролитие гадостью is a contrast to the previous clause: теперь же 'now, however', мы 'we', хоть и is a concessive conjunction 'although / even though', считаем 'consider' (1st pl. present), кровопролитие is the accusative noun 'bloodshed', and гадостью is the instrumental predicative 'an abomination / vile thing' (we call it that).
segments.p1-7-g251: а все-таки этой гадостью занимаемся continues the contrast: а 'but', все-таки 'nevertheless / after all' is a particle, этой гадостью is instrumental 'with/ in this abomination' (i.e. we occupy ourselves with it), and занимаемся is the reflexive verb 'we engage in / are occupied with' (1st pl. present).
segments.p1-7-g252: да еще больше is an intensifying phrase: да here works as a colloquial 'and / moreover', ещё 'still / even / more', and больше 'more' — together 'and even more'.
segments.p1-7-g253: чем прежде closes the comparative: чем 'than' and прежде 'before / formerly', meaning 'more than before.'
segments.p1-7-g254: Что хуже? is an exclamatory/elliptical question: Что 'what' (exclamatory), хуже is the comparative adjective 'worse' — literally 'what is worse?' or 'what follows is worse'.
segments.p1-7-g272: 'Вы уверены,' is a direct address: 'Вы' is 2nd-person (plural/formal) and 'уверены' is the short-form adjective used predicatively (plural agreement), meaning 'are sure'. The comma introduces the following subordinate clause.
segments.p1-7-g273: The subordinate clause begins with the conjunction 'что' ('that'); 'тогда' is a temporal adverb 'then'. 'человек' is the subject 'a man/person'. The sequence 'и сам' places emphasis ('and/even himself'); 'перестанет' is future tense (perfective) meaning 'will cease/stop' and takes the infinitive 'ошибаться' ('to make mistakes'). 'добровольно' modifies the verb. The final 'и' is a coordinating conjunction linking the next clause.
segments.p1-7-g274: 'так сказать' is a parenthetical fixed phrase meaning 'so to speak' or 'as one might say'; here it flags the upcoming figurative or hedged formulation.
segments.p1-7-g275: 'поневоле' is an adverb meaning 'against one's will' or 'involuntarily'. The verb 'захочет' is future (perfective of 'захотеть') and is negated by 'не' ('will not want'). 'роднить' is a verb 'to make kin/to unite (figuratively)': it takes a direct object ('свою волю') and the preposition 'с' with an instrumental phrase ('нормальными своими интересами'). Note that 'нормальными своими интересами' places the adjective and possessive before the noun in the instrumental plural.
segments.p1-7-g276: 'Мало того:' is an idiomatic transitional phrase meaning 'moreover' or 'what is more'; grammatically it's 'мало' + genitive 'того' used as a fixed connective, often followed by a colon.
segments.p1-7-g277: 'тогда' is a temporal adverb 'then/in that case', marking the time reference for the following reported statement.
segments.p1-7-g278: 'говорите вы,' is reported speech: 'говорите' is present tense, 2nd-person plural 'you say', with the pronoun 'вы' added for emphasis or formality. The inversion (verb before pronoun) is neutral in reported phrases.
segments.p1-7-g279: 'сама наука научит человека' — 'сама' ('itself') emphasizes the noun 'наука' (science). 'научит' is future perfective 'will teach' and takes the accusative 'человека' as direct object. The parenthetical 'хоть это уж и роскошь' begins here (closed in the next segment).
segments.p1-7-g280: 'по-моему' is a fixed parenthetical meaning 'in my opinion' formed from the preposition 'по' + dative possessive 'моему'. It closes the parenthesis.
segments.p1-7-g281: The clause begins with 'что' ('that'). 'ни воли,' uses the particle 'ни' as part of the correlative 'ни... ни...' meaning 'neither... nor...', and 'воли' (genitive of 'воля') means 'will' (capacity to will/do).
segments.p1-7-g282: 'ни каприза на самом-то деле у него и нет,' continues the 'neither... nor...' construction: 'каприза' is 'caprice/whim' (genitive). 'на самом-то деле' is an emphatic adverbial phrase 'in reality' (with the enclitic particle '-то' adding emphasis). 'у него нет' is the existential/possessive pattern 'he does not have', with 'и' used here as an intensifier.
segments.p1-7-g283: 'да и никогда не бывало,' is a follow-up emphatic clause: 'да' here functions roughly as 'and/in fact', 'и' is connective, 'никогда' = 'never', and 'не бывало' (impersonal perfective) = 'has never happened/occurred'.
segments.p1-7-g284: 'а что он сам не более,' introduces a contrastive clause with 'а' = 'but/and'. 'он сам' ('he himself') is emphasized, and the idiom 'не более' means 'no more than' (limiting comparison).
segments.p1-7-g285: 'как нечто вроде фортепьянной клавиши или органного штифтика' is a simile introduced by 'как'/'вроде' meaning 'like' or 'something like'. The nouns are descriptive images in the appropriate forms ('фортепьянной клавиши' = 'piano key', 'органного штифтика' = 'organ peg/pin').
segments.p1-7-g286: 'и что,' is a simple coordinator 'and that' introducing the next proposition; both words are standard conjunctions.
segments.p1-7-g287: 'сверх того,' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'besides' or 'in addition' — 'сверх' = 'beyond/in addition to' plus genitive 'того'.
segments.p1-7-g288: 'на свете есть еще законы природы;' is an existential statement: 'на свете' = 'in the world', 'есть' = existential 'there are', 'еще' = 'also/still', and 'законы природы' = 'laws of nature'.
segments.p1-7-g289: 'так что все,' begins the result clause: 'так что' = 'so that/therefore', and 'все' = 'everything/everyone' introducing what follows as a general consequence.
segments.p1-7-g290: 'что он ни делает,' is a concessive formula meaning 'whatever he does' — 'ни' after 'что' creates the 'no matter what' sense; 'делает' is present-tense 'does'.
segments.p1-7-g291: 'делается вовсе не по его хотенью,' uses the impersonal/passive 'делается' ('is done/happens'), 'вовсе не' = 'not at all', and 'по его хотенью' = 'according to his desire' (archaic noun 'хотенье' used with 'по' meaning 'by/according to his wish').
segments.p1-7-g292: 'а само собою,' is a short phrase meaning 'but by itself' or 'of itself' — 'само' = 'itself', 'собою' = 'by/with itself; of itself'. It contrasts human intention with autonomous occurrence.
segments.p1-7-g293: 'по законам природы.' closes the sentence: 'по' = 'according to', governing the dative/plural 'законам' ('laws'), and 'природы' = 'of nature' (genitive) — 'according to the laws of nature'.
segments.p1-7-g294: Следственно is an adverb/conjunctive adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore,' introducing the logical result of the previous statement.
segments.p1-7-g295: эти законы природы = 'these laws of nature' (эти = 'these'); стоит только открыть is an impersonal construction: стоит + infinitive means 'it is enough to / all that remains is to' (here 'only need to discover'); открыть is infinitive 'to discover/reveal.'
segments.p1-7-g296: и links the result clause. уж here gives a sense of 'by then/indeed' (emphatic); за поступки свои = 'for his/one's deeds' (за = 'for' marking responsibility); человек отвечать не будет = 'a person will not have to answer/will not be responsible' (отвечать за = 'to be accountable for'); и жить ему будет чрезвычайно легко = 'and living will be extraordinarily easy for him' — жить is infinitive used impersonally, ему is dative 'for him', and будет + adverb + adjective gives 'will be extremely easy.'
segments.p1-7-g297: A noun phrase in the nominative plural: 'Все' (a universal quantifier) modifies 'поступки человеческие' (deeds/actions pertaining to humans). The word order ('поступки человеческие' rather than the more neutral 'человеческие поступки') is slightly emphatic or literary.
segments.p1-7-g298: 'само собою' is an idiomatic parenthetical adverbial meaning 'by itself' / 'of course' (literary). Grammatically it functions as a comment on the clause that follows; 'собою' is an older/instrumental form of 'собой'.
segments.p1-7-g299: Passive future construction: 'будут' (future auxiliary) + past passive participle 'расчислены' = 'will be calculated'. 'тогда' is a temporal/conditional adverb 'then/in that case'. 'по этим законам' uses the preposition 'по' + dative plural ('этим законам') meaning 'according to these laws'.
