segments.p1-7-g300: Adverb 'математически' ('mathematically') modifies the preceding passive predicate and indicates the manner of calculation; set off by commas as a parenthetical manner adverb.
segments.p1-7-g301: 'вроде' + genitive phrase here means 'like' or 'similar to'; 'таблицы логарифмов' is a genitive construction 'a table of logarithms' functioning as the object of comparison.
segments.p1-7-g302: The preposition 'до' with a numeral indicates an upper limit: 'up to 108,000' (i.e., enumerated or indexed as high as 108,000).
segments.p1-7-g303: Continues the passive predicates: the verb 'будут' from earlier also governs 'занесены' (past passive participle) so together they mean 'will be ... and entered'. 'в календарь' is accusative with 'в' indicating entry/recording into the calendar.
segments.p1-7-g304: A small parenthetical turn: 'или ещё лучше того' = 'or better than that / better yet'; 'или' links alternatives, 'ещё' intensifies, 'лучше' is comparative 'better' used adverbially, and 'того' refers back to the foregoing idea (genitive demonstrative) in an idiomatic phrase.
segments.p1-7-g305: 'появятся' is the simple future (intransitive) third person plural 'will appear/turn up'. The following noun phrase 'некоторые благонамеренные издания' is nominative plural (some well-meaning publications) and is the subject of 'появятся'.
segments.p1-7-g306: 'вроде' again introduces an example/simile; 'теперешних энциклопедических лексиконов' is genitive plural 'of the present/modern encyclopedic lexicons', giving the kind of publications meant.
segments.p1-7-g307: Relative clause introduced by 'в которых' ('in which', referring to the editions): 'все' here means 'everything' (collective) and governs the neuter singular predicate 'будет исчислено и обозначено' — two past participles used in a passive future construction with 'будет' ('will be reckoned and designated'). 'так точно' is an adverbial phrase 'so exactly'.
segments.p1-7-g308: 'что' introduces the result clause: 'that in the world there will no longer be...'. 'на свете' is a fixed locative expression 'in the world'. The stacked negation 'уже не будет более' yields 'will no longer be' (ужe + не + более = no longer). The phrase sets up the first half of a correlative negative pair with 'ни ... ни'.
segments.p1-7-g309: Completes the correlative negative pair 'ни ... ни' = 'neither ... nor'; 'приключений' is genitive plural governed by the negative existential 'не будет' and means 'adventures/incidents'.
segments.p1-7-g339: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning 'after all' used to introduce or emphasize the remark. The clause uses the short-form adjective глуп (short predicate form of глупый) before the noun человек; Russian allows adjective-predicate + noun word order with no copula in the present, so this reads literally like 'After all, stupid [is] a person.' The short form глуп is masculine singular and functions as the predicative describing человек.
segments.p1-7-g340: The adjective глуп appears again in short predicative form for emphasis, and феноменально is an adverb modifying that predicate, meaning 'phenomenally' (degree). The sentence repeats the predicate elliptically (no subject repeated) to intensify the claim.
segments.p1-7-g341: То есть is a fixed phrase meaning 'that is' or 'that is to say' (То = 'that', есть = copula 'is' in this idiom). Он is the subject 'he'. Хоть и introduces a concessive clause ('though/although'), with и here adding emphasis; вовсе не is a strong negation 'not at all', and глуп is the short-form adjective predicate 'stupid'.
segments.p1-7-g342: Но introduces contrast ('but'), уж and зато together ('уж зато') add emphatic contrast — 'indeed/on the other hand'. Неблагодарен is the short-form adjective predicate 'ungrateful'. Так here is an adverb of degree 'so', preparing the result clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-7-g343: Что introduces a result clause ('so that/that'), поискать is a perfective infinitive 'to try to find / to look for' used impersonally in expressions like 'поискать другого' and другой in the genitive (другого) means 'another [one]'. The clause sets up the impossibility expressed next.
segments.p1-7-g344: Так here repeats the adverb 'so' (linking to the previous 'так... что' construction). Не + infinitive найти expresses inability or impossibility in a general/impersonal sense: 'not to find' = 'you won't find'. The clause is elliptical in Russian (literal: 'so not find').
segments.p1-7-g345: Ведь is a discourse particle ('after all' / 'you see') used to emphasize what follows; я is the first-person pronoun 'I' and is the subject of the verbs to follow.
segments.p1-7-g346: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example' that signals the following statement is an instance or illustration. It stands alone and is separated by commas.
segments.p1-7-g347: Нисколько (with the negation не) forms the idiom 'нисколько не удивлюсь' meaning 'I shall not be at all surprised'. Удивлюсь is the future tense, 1st person singular of удивиться ('to be surprised').
segments.p1-7-g348: Если introduces a conditional 'if'; вдруг 'suddenly'; ни с того ни с сего is an idiom meaning 'out of nowhere' (literally 'not from that nor from this'); среди всеобщего будущего благоразумия is a prepositional phrase 'amid the universal future prudence' (all adjectives in genitive to match благоразумия). Возникнет is future 3rd person singular of возникнуть 'will arise/appear'; какой-нибудь is an indefinite pronoun 'some/any' modifying джентльмен; с неблагородной attaches to джентльмен and describes him 'with a dishonorable...'.
segments.p1-7-g349: Лучше сказать is a parenthetical corrective phrase meaning 'better to say' or 'rather (to say)'; it signals the author is revising the previous descriptor.
segments.p1-7-g350: С + instrumental (ретроградной ... физиономией) means 'with a ... physiognomy/face'. Ретроградной is an adjective (reactionary/retrograde) in the instrumental; насмешливою is an adverbial/epithet in an older orthographic/inflexional form (насмешливою vs modern насмешливой) meaning 'mocking' and agrees in case and gender; физиономией is the instrumental of 'physiognomy/face'.
segments.p1-7-g351: Упрет (3rd sing. future of упереть/упереться) here means 'will plant/press' (his hands) — colloquial for 'stick his hands on his hips'. Руки (accusative) 'hands', в боки is the direction 'into/onto his sides' (on his hips). И connects the coordinated verb скажет ('will say'), which takes нам всем (dative + целое) 'to all of us' as its indirect object.
segments.p1-7-g352: А что is a colloquial discourse opener here (а = 'and/well', что functioning as an exclamatory/introductory particle) that launches the quoted rhetorical question that follows.
segments.p1-7-g353: Господа is a vocative/plural address 'gentlemen' (here used to address the assembled hearers directly).
segments.p1-7-g354: Не ... ли is the interrogative particle that turns the infinitive into a rhetorical question ('shall we not...?'). Столкнуть is the infinitive 'to topple/knock down' (here used with meaning 'push off/overturn'); нам in this construction is the dative implying 'for us' or the sense 'shall we (us) ...'; все это благоразумие is the direct object 'all this prudence'; с одного разу is an adverbial phrase meaning 'all at once' or 'in one fell swoop' (literally 'from one time').
segments.p1-7-g355: Ногой is the instrumental singular of нога 'with the foot' and indicates the means ('with a foot' = 'by stamping/kicking').
segments.p1-7-g356: Прахом is the instrumental of прах 'dust/ashes' and here is used figuratively to mean 'to dust/to powder' — i.e., 'grind into dust'.
segments.p1-7-g357: Единственно 'only/solely' qualifies the purpose expressed by the following noun phrase; с тою целью is an idiomatic instrumental phrase 'with that purpose' (тою is an older demonstrative instrumental form of 'that'); целью is the instrumental of цель 'purpose/aim'.
segments.p1-7-g358: Чтоб introduces purpose/result clauses ('so that/in order that'). Все эти логарифмы is the object 'all these logarithms' (logarithms are used here satirically for abstract calculations or cold reason). Отправились is past/passive-like perfective plural (отправиться) 'be sent off / depart' (here 'be sent to'); к черту is an idiom 'to the devil'. The repeated чтоб introduces the second parallel purpose 'and that we might again ...'; нам is dative 'for us', опять 'again', по своей воле is an expression meaning 'according to one's own will' (по + dative + воля), глупой qualifying воле, and пожить (infinitive) 'to live (for a while)' — here: 'to live again by our own foolish will.'
segments.p1-7-g359: Это 'this/that' is the subject; бы is the conditional particle forming 'this would be'; ещё ничего is an idiom meaning 'would be nothing / would be no big deal' (еще here softens the assessment). The whole phrase expresses that the preceding hypothetical would, in itself, not be problematic.
segments.p1-7-g360: Но 'but' introduces the contrast; обидно is a predicative adjective meaning 'it's offensive/annoying' or 'what is annoying'; то is a demonstrative pronoun 'that' pointing forward to the explanatory clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-7-g361: Что introduces the explanatory clause 'that ...'; ведь adds colloquial emphasis 'after all'; непременно 'certainly/without fail' modifies the verb; последователей (accusative plural) is the object 'followers'; найдет is future 3rd person singular of найти 'will find'. The phrase says it will inevitably attract followers.
segments.p1-7-g362: Так 'thus/so' summarizes the previous statement; человек is the generic noun 'man/person/people'; устроен is a short passive/predicative ('is arranged/made/composed') used to state a general fact about human nature: 'people are made this way.'
segments.p1-7-g363: A short, left‑dislocated clause meaning 'And all this (is) from the most trivial reason'; Russian omits the copula 'is'. 'самой пустейшей' is a superlative/pejorative modifier (very/utterly trivial).
segments.p1-7-g364: 'об которой бы' is the preposition + relative pronoun with the particle 'бы' that softens/weakens the assertion; together it starts a parenthetical remark 'about which, (one might say)...'.
segments.p1-7-g365: Parenthetical impersonal verb 'кажется' = 'it seems' or 'one would think' — marks the speaker's tentative view.
segments.p1-7-g366: Impersonal modal construction 'и упоминать не стоит' = 'and it is not worth mentioning'; 'стоит' here functions like English 'is worth' (impersonal + infinitive omitted).
segments.p1-7-g367: 'именно оттого' = 'precisely because' — a connective (adv + compound preposition) introducing the explanatory clause that follows.
segments.p1-7-g368: Subordinating conjunction 'что' ('that') plus subject 'человек' ('man/person') — begins the reason clause.
segments.p1-7-g369: Adverbial phrase 'всегда и везде' = 'always and everywhere', a generalizing time/place qualifier for the following statement.
segments.p1-7-g370: Concessive relative clause 'кто бы он ни был' = 'whoever he may be'; 'бы' is the subjunctive/conditional particle used with 'ни' to form a concessive ('no matter who').
segments.p1-7-g371: 'любил действовать так' — 'liked to act in this way'; 'любил' here describes a habitual inclination (past/present generalization), 'действовать' is the infinitive 'to act', 'так' = 'that way'.
segments.p1-7-g372: 'как хотел' = 'as he wanted'; 'хотел' is past tense of 'want' used to report the subject's preference.
segments.p1-7-g373: Contrastive particle 'а' introduces the opposite case: 'and not at all so'; 'вовсе' strengthens the negation 'не так' = 'not at all in that way'.
segments.p1-7-g374: 'как повелевали ему разум и выгода' = 'as reason and advantage ordered him'; 'повелевали' is past plural of 'to command/order' agreeing with the plural subject 'разум и выгода'.
segments.p1-7-g375: 'хотеть же можно и против собственной выгоды' — 'one can want even against one's own interest'; 'можно' is impersonal 'it is possible', 'же' is an emphatic/contrastive particle, 'против' = 'against'.
segments.p1-7-g376: 'а иногда и положительно должно (это уж моя идея)' = 'and sometimes indeed one positively ought to (that's my idea)'; 'должно' is the modal 'ought to', 'положительно' adds force ('positively/certainly'), and the parenthesis marks the narrator's personal remark.
segments.p1-7-g377: 'Свое собственное' is a doubled possessive adjective construction meaning 'one's very own' — emphatic possession of what follows.
segments.p1-7-g378: 'вольное и свободное хотенье' = 'a volitional/willful and free desire'; two near‑synonymous adjectives stress autonomy; 'хотенье' is a nominal form of 'want/desire'.
segments.p1-7-g379: Another possessive pair 'свой собственный' = 'one's own, proper' — repetition increases emphasis on ownership.
segments.p1-7-g380: 'хотя бы самый дикий каприз' = 'even the very wildest caprice'; 'хотя бы' here means 'even', 'самый' intensifies to 'the very/most', 'дикий каприз' = 'wild whim'.
segments.p1-7-g381: 'своя фантазия' = 'one's own imagination' — simple possessive noun phrase parallel to the previous items.
segments.p1-7-g382: Participial phrase modifying 'фантазия': 'раздраженная иногда хоть бы даже до сумасшествия' = 'sometimes irritated even to the point of madness'; 'раздраженная' is a past participle, 'хоть бы даже' intensifies the concession 'even'.
segments.p1-7-g383: Emphatic resumptive phrase 'вот это-то все и есть та самая' = 'well, this very thing is precisely the one...' — 'то' and the particles give strong pointing emphasis.
segments.p1-7-g384: Single adjective/participle 'пропущенная' = 'omitted/left out' describing the following noun 'выгода' (the gain that has been skipped over).
segments.p1-7-g385: 'самая выгодная выгода' = 'the most advantageous advantage' (style intentionally tautological): 'самая' is superlative intensifier, 'выгодная выгода' stresses that this gain is profitable in its own (paradoxical) way.
segments.p1-7-g386: Relative clause: 'которая ни под какую классификацию не подходит' = 'which does not fit under any classification' ('ни...какую' = 'no/any'); 'и от которой все системы и теории постоянно разлетаются к черту' = 'and from which all systems and theories constantly fly apart to the devil' (idiomatic: collapse/disintegrate).
segments.p1-7-g387: 'И с чего это взяли все эти мудрецы' is an idiomatic question 'And what did all these wise men take that from?' = 'what made them think that'; 'взяли' here means 'assumed/inferred'.
segments.p1-7-g388: Content clause 'что человеку надо какого-то нормального' = 'that a man needs some kind of normal (desire)'; 'надо' is the impersonal modal 'needs/should', 'какого-то' is an indefinite 'some kind of'.
segments.p1-7-g389: Continuation 'какого-то добродетельного хотения?' — 'some kind of virtuous desire?' The question underscores the narrator's incredulity about such prescriptive wants.
segments.p1-7-g390: 'С чего это непременно вообразили они' = 'What on earth made them imagine (so certainly)?' — 'непременно' intensifies the incredulity, 'вообразили' = 'imagined/supposed'.
segments.p1-7-g391: Reported content: 'что человеку надо непременно благоразумно выгодного хотенья?' = 'that a man must necessarily have a prudently advantageous desire?'; 'благоразумно' is an adverb modifying the adjective 'выгодного' (i.e. prudently advantageous).
segments.p1-7-g117: Приготовляясь is a деепричастие (adverbial participle) formed from приготовляться — 'preparing' (continuous), introducing a circumstance simultaneous to the main action and modifying the subject of the main clause; к делу is a prepositional phrase 'to/for the matter' (literal 'to the affair').
segments.p1-7-g118: этот господин is the subject 'this gentleman'; тотчас же is an adverbial phrase meaning 'at once / immediately' with же adding emphasis; изложит is the perfective future 3rd-person singular of изложить 'will set forth / will state'; вам is the dative 'to you'.
segments.p1-7-g119: велеречиво и ясно are adverbial modifiers of изложит: велеречиво (somewhat archaic) = 'eloquently / grandiloquently' and ясно = 'clearly'; they qualify the manner of his exposition.
segments.p1-7-g120: как именно is a phrase 'exactly how'; надо is an impersonal modal 'it is necessary / one ought to'; ему (dative) indicates who must act ('for him' / 'he ought to'); поступить is the infinitive 'to act/proceed'; по законам рассудка и истины is an instrumental/according-to phrase 'according to the laws/principles of reason and truth' that governs how he should act.
segments.p1-7-g121: Мало того is an idiomatic connective 'moreover / that's not all' (literally 'little of that'), used to introduce an additional point.
segments.p1-7-g122: с волнением и страстью is an adverbial phrase 'with excitement and passion'; будет говорить is a future construction 'will speak' (будет + imperfective) indicating action; вам is dative 'to you'; о introduces the topic 'about'; настоящих begins the noun phrase 'real/genuine ...' that continues in the next segment.
segments.p1-7-g123: нормальных человеческих интересах completes the prepositional object: нормальных = 'normal' (qualifying), человеческих = 'human' (qualifying), интересах is the prepositional plural of интерес 'interests/concerns' (here: stakes or legitimate human concerns).
segments.p1-7-g124: с насмешкой укорит is 'with mockery he will reproach/upbraid' (укорит is perfective future/present 3sg of укорить, here future in narrative); близоруких глупцов is the accusative plural 'near-sighted fools' (близоруких = short-sighted, глупцов = fools).
segments.p1-7-g125: не понимающих is a present active participle 'not understanding' modifying глупцов; ни своих выгод uses the correlative ни ... ни ... meaning 'neither ... nor ...', своих выгод = 'their own advantages/interests' (выгод here = advantages/interests).
segments.p1-7-g126: ни настоящего значения добродетели continues the correlative 'nor the true significance/meaning of virtue': настоящего = 'true/real', значения = 'meaning / significance', добродетели = 'virtue' (genitive).
segments.p1-7-g127: и - ровно через четверть часа is a temporal adverbial 'and — exactly/precisely in/after a quarter of an hour'; ровно = 'exactly', через + time period = 'in / after [time]'.
segments.p1-7-g128: без всякого внезапного is a prepositional phrase 'without any sudden [cause]' — без = 'without', всякого = 'any', внезапного = 'sudden' (adjective).
segments.p1-7-g129: постороннего повода continues 'external/irrelevant occasion/reason': постороннего = 'extraneous / external', повода = genitive of повод 'reason/occasion'.
segments.p1-7-g130: а именно по чему-то такому внутреннему is a clarifying phrase 'namely because of something of that inner sort': а именно = 'namely / that is', по + dative here means 'on account of / because of', чему-то такому внутреннему = 'something (of) that kind, inner/psychological'.
segments.p1-7-g131: что сильнее всех его интересов is a relative clause 'which is stronger than all his interests': что = 'which', сильнее = comparative 'stronger/more powerful', всех его интересов = 'of all his interests' (genitive).
segments.p1-7-g132: - выкинет совершенно другое колено is an idiomatic clause: выкинет (perfective future) literally 'will throw out' but here 'will suddenly do/take a different turn'; совершенно = 'completely', другое колено = literally 'a different knee/segment' (figurative: a different tack/turn).
segments.p1-7-g133: то есть явно пойдет против того introduces the explanation 'that is, will clearly go against that [which...]': то есть = 'that is', явно = 'clearly', пойдет = future of пойти 'will go / will act', против того = 'against that'.
segments.p1-7-g134: об чем сам говорил is a colloquial equivalent of 'о чем' meaning 'about which he himself had spoken': об = 'about/concerning', чем = 'which/what', сам = emphatic 'himself', говорил = past tense 'said/spoke'.
segments.p1-7-g135: и против законов рассудка continues the list 'and against the laws of reason': и = 'and', против = 'against', законов = 'laws/principles' (genitive), рассудка = 'of reason/judgment'.
segments.p1-7-g136: и против собственной выгоды adds 'and against his own advantage': собственной = 'one's own' (reflexive possessive), выгоды = 'advantage/interest'.
segments.p1-7-g137: ну is a short interjection 'well' used to introduce or soften the following summary remark.
segments.p1-7-g138: одним словом is a set phrase 'in a word / in short' used to summarize.
segments.p1-7-g139: против всего is the concluding generalization 'against everything' (всего here 'everything' in the genitive after против).
segments.p1-7-g142: Предупрежду is 1st-person singular (literary) 'I warn' / 'I shall warn' — the narrator's verb of address; it's a simple declarative verb.
segments.p1-7-g143: что introduces the clause 'that'; мой приятель = 'my friend'; лицо собирательное = 'a collective person/figure' (лицо = 'person/figure' here, собирательное = 'collective/aggregative'), i.e. the friend represents a type rather than a single individual.
segments.p1-7-g144: и потому only его одного винить как-то трудно is an impersonal construction 'and therefore it is somewhat hard to blame only him alone': и потому = 'and therefore', только его одного = emphatic 'only him alone', винить = infinitive 'to blame', как-то = 'somehow / to some extent', трудно = 'difficult'.
segments.p1-7-g145: То-то и есть is an emphatic idiom 'That is exactly the point' (repetition of то intensifies the demonstrative; есть = 'is').
segments.p1-7-g146: господа is the vocative/plural address 'gentlemen' — the narrator addressing his audience.
segments.p1-7-g147: не существует ли is a rhetorical question 'doesn't there exist / is there not', with ли marking the interrogative; и в самом деле adds 'indeed / really'; нечто такое = 'something like that / some such thing'.
segments.p1-7-g148: что почти всякому человеку дороже самых лучших его выгод is a relative clause 'which is, to almost every person, dearer than his very best advantages': почти = 'almost', всякому человеку = dative 'to every/any person', дороже = comparative 'dearer', самых лучших его выгод = 'of his very best advantages' (superlative phrase).
segments.p1-7-g149: или (чтоб уж логики не нарушать) есть одна такая самая выгодная выгода is a parenthetical qualification 'or (so as not to offend the logicians) there is one such most advantageous advantage' — the clause playfully repeats выгод/выгода (interest/advantage) and uses самая to mark superlative emphasis; пропускаемая-то that follows is 'the very one omitted/overlooked' with the particle -то adding emphasis.
segments.p1-7-g150: вот об которой сейчас говорили is a clarifying parenthesis 'the very one we were just talking about': вот = 'here/that very', об которой = 'about which', сейчас говорили = 'were just speaking'.
segments.p1-7-g151: которая главнее и выгоднее всех других выгод и для которой человек introduces a relative clause 'which is more important and more advantageous than all other advantages, and for which a person ...': главнее and выгоднее are comparatives modifying которая; для которой = 'for which'.
segments.p1-7-g152: если понадобится is a conditional clause 'if it becomes necessary / if need arises' (понадобится is impersonal future 'will be needed').
segments.p1-7-g153: готов против всех законов пойти states 'ready/willing to go against all laws': готов = 'ready/willing', против всех законов = 'against all laws', пойти = infinitive 'to go' (here 'to act contrary to').
segments.p1-7-g154: то есть против рассудка is an explanatory phrase 'that is, against reason': то есть = 'that is', против рассудка = 'against reason/judgment'.
segments.p1-7-g155: чести is the genitive 'honour' — part of the list of things one would go against.
segments.p1-7-g156: покоя is the genitive 'peace/quiet' — continuing the list.
segments.p1-7-g157: благоденствия is the genitive 'well‑being / prosperity' — continuing the enumerated goods one would sacrifice.
segments.p1-7-g158: - одним словом is the summarizing tag 'in a word / in short' that introduces the final summary.
segments.p1-7-g159: против всех этих прекрасных и полезных вещей lists 'against all these beautiful and useful things' (прекрасных = 'beautiful/excellent', полезных = 'useful/beneficial', вещей = 'things/matters').
segments.p1-7-g160: лишь бы только достигнуть этой первоначальной is a wish/condition 'provided only that [he] attain this initial/primary ...' where лишь бы (with бы) expresses 'if only / so long as', достигнуть = 'to attain', этой первоначальной = 'this original/primary (one)'.
segments.p1-7-g161: самой выгодной выгоды completes the object: самой = 'the very / most', выгодной = 'advantageous', выгоды = 'advantage/interest' — rhetorical tautology emphasizing that particular interest.
segments.p1-7-g162: которая ему дороже всего is the final relative clause 'which is dearer to him than anything else': которая = 'which', ему = dative 'to him', дороже = comparative 'dearer', всего = 'of all / than anything' (superlative sense).

sentences.p1-7-s19: After all, your benefits are well-being, wealth, freedom, peace, and so on and so on; so that a man who, for example, would openly and arrogantly go against this whole register would be, in your view — well, and certainly in mine — an obscurantist or quite mad, is that so?

sentences.p1-7-s36: "Allow me," we shall still explain ourselves, and it's not a pun; rather, the point is that this advantage is remarkable precisely because it destroys all our classifications and constantly shatters all the systems devised by well-meaning people of the human race for the happiness of mankind.

sentences.p1-7-s71: Consequently, these laws of nature only need to be discovered, and then a person will no longer be held responsible for his deeds, and living will be extraordinarily easy for him.
sentences.p1-7-s72: All human actions, of course, will then be calculated according to those laws, mathematically, like a table of logarithms, up to 108,000, and entered in a calendar; or, better yet, some well-meaning publications will appear, like today's encyclopedic lexicons, in which everything will be so exactly reckoned and designated that there will no longer be any deeds or adventures in the world.
sentences.p1-7-s83: After all, a person is stupid — phenomenally stupid.

sentences.p1-7-s85: For my part, I, for example, would not be at all surprised if, all of a sudden and out of nowhere, amid the universal future prudence some gentleman should appear with a dishonorable — or, better said, with a reactionary and mocking — countenance, plant his hands on his hips and say to us all: 'Well then, gentlemen, shall we not topple all this prudence at once with our foot, grind it to dust, solely for the purpose that all these logarithms be sent to the devil and that we may once again live according to our own foolish will?''
sentences.p1-7-s86: That might be nothing, but what is upsetting is that, after all, it will certainly find followers — that's how people are made.
sentences.p1-7-s87: And all this is for the most trivial reason, about which, it would seem, it is not even worth mentioning: precisely because man, always and everywhere, whoever he may be, liked to act as he wanted and not at all as reason and advantage ordered him; and one can want even against one's own advantage, and sometimes indeed ought to (that’s my idea).
sentences.p1-7-s88: One's own, voluntary and free desire; one's very own, even the wildest caprice; one's imagination, sometimes roused even to madness — that, in fact, is the very omitted, most advantageous gain which does not fit any classification and from which all systems and theories constantly fly to the devil.

sentences.p1-7-s29: While preparing to act, this gentleman will immediately set forth to you, eloquently and clearly, exactly how he ought to proceed according to the laws of reason and truth.
sentences.p1-7-s30: Moreover: with excitement and passion he will speak to you of real, normal human interests; with mockery he will reproach near-sighted fools who understand neither their own advantages nor the true meaning of virtue; and — exactly a quarter of an hour later, without any sudden external occasion, namely on account of something inner that is stronger than all his interests — he will take a completely different tack, that is, will plainly act against what he himself had said: both against the laws of reason and against his own advantage, well, in a word, against everything.
sentences.p1-7-s33: I warn you that my 'friend' is a collective figure, and therefore it's somewhat hard to blame him alone.
sentences.p1-7-s34: That's precisely it, gentlemen: isn't there really something that is to almost every person dearer than his very best interests, or (so as not to offend the logicians) is there one such most advantageous interest (the very one being passed over — the one we were just talking about) that is more important and more advantageous than all other interests and for which a man, if necessary, is ready to go against all laws, that is, against reason, honour, peace, prosperity — in short, against all those fine and useful things, provided only that he attain this primary, most advantageous interest which is dearer to him than anything else?
